raw_output:
Alright, listen close now. First thing—make sure the horse's got good health and you're clear on the ground. Don't rush. Start by walking the horse slowly near the ground so you get used to its movement. When you're ready, step up with your front foot first, keep your knees bent, and grab the reins lightly. Let the horse move naturally—don't pull too hard. 

Once you're on, keep your weight centered over the horse's back, not too far forward or back. Your hands should be loose but firm, guiding the horse with your body, not your arms. Always listen to what the horse says—its breath, its steps. If it's nervous, slow down. If it's happy, let it carry you. And remember—never let your balance slip. Keep your eyes forward, stay calm. 

Riding's all about trust and patience. Don't try to force it. Just move with the horse, not against it. That's how you keep 'em safe and you stay steady. Now go get that horse and show it what you got.
